item.title: Fujitsu Falls Most in 11 Years After Forecast Misses Estimates
item.description: Fujitsu Ltd. shares fell as much as 14%, the most in 11 years, after the company’s fourth-quarter operating profit and full-year forecast missed analyst estimates.

item.title: Japan’s Factory Output Falls as Iran War Clouds Demand Outlook
item.description: Japan’s factory output fell as the war in Iran clouded prospects for global demand while also threatening manufacturers’ margins by way of higher energy costs.

item.title: Australia’s Top Migrant Group Hails From India, Trumping England
item.description: Indians are now Australia’s largest migrant group, supplanting the English for the first time ever, in a change that highlights the rise of immigration as an increasingly contentious political issue.

item.title: Samsung’s Chip Profit Soars 48-Fold Due to AI Spending Frenzy
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co.’s semiconductor arm beat expectations with a 48-fold jump in profit after AI’s reliance on memory delivered hefty margins.

item.title: SoftBank to Create and List AI Firm Roze in the US, FT Says
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp. plans to establish and float a standalone AI robotics and data center company called Roze in the US, the Financial Times reported, citing unidentified people familiar with the matter.

item.title: ASX Says Darren Yip to Become Interim CEO as Search Goes On
item.description: ASX Ltd. named Darren Yip, who oversees the exchange operator’s markets and listings divisions, as its interim boss amid a broader strategic review at the firm to lift risk management and governance practices.

item.title: Hong Kong’s Local Debt Market Becomes Surprise Funding Hotspot
item.description: Hong Kong’s once-quiet local currency bond market is quickly becoming one of Asia’s hottest funding venues, as corporate issuers seek stability in a city looking to lure more debt offerings.

item.title: RBNZ to Disclose Votes of Monetary Policy Committee Members
item.description: New Zealand’s central bank will disclose the votes of members of its Monetary Policy Committee if it is unable to reach a consensus decision on interest rates under the terms of a new charter agreed with the government.

item.title: Top US Apartment Owners AvalonBay, EQR to Discuss Combining
item.description: AvalonBay Communities Inc. and Equity Residential are considering a potential combination that could reshape the US apartment market, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Japan’s Two-Year Bond Sale Tests Demand After BOJ’s Hawkish Hold
item.description: Japan’s two-year bond auction on Thursday will test investor demand after recent policy signals from the Bank of Japan and the Federal Reserve added upward pressure to short-term rates.

item.title: Warsh Set to Inherit a Fed That Looks Ready to Fight Rate Cuts
item.description: As Kevin Warsh moves closer to becoming chair of the Federal Reserve, his future colleagues are moving further from supporting the resumption of interest rate cuts that President Donald Trump is expecting.

item.title: AI-Led Rally in Asia Stocks Masks Deeper Damage From Iran War
item.description: Asia’s artificial intelligence-fueled rally is masking signs of strain, with gains in tech names overshadowing the impact of the US-Iran war on the broader market.

item.title: Gold Steadies After Fed Holds Rates and Signals Inflation Risks
item.description: Gold held losses after the US Federal Reserve kept interest rates steady and said the war in Iran was clouding the economic outlook.

item.title: Starwood’s SREIT Suspends Redemptions While It Waits for Growth
item.description: Starwood Capital Group is halting redemptions from one of its real estate investment trusts in a bid to support the fund’s share price and performance.

item.title: Oil Extends Rally as Hormuz Stays Shut With Blockade to Continue
item.description: Oil pushed higher for a fourth session, with no signs of progress toward a resolution to the Iran war that’s roiled global markets following the near-closure of the crucial Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: US Stock Futures Fall on Tech Earnings, Oil Gains: Markets Wrap
item.description: US equity-index futures fell early Thursday after a mixed picture from tech giants reporting late Wednesday in the US failed to ease concerns over AI spending.

item.title: Japan’s Record Sales of Euro Bonds Show Historic Funding Shift
item.description: Japanese borrowers are selling record amounts of euro-denominated bonds in one of the most prominent examples of global companies scaling back their reliance on the dollar.

item.title: Anthropic Considering Funding Offers at Over $900 Billion Value
item.description: Anthropic PBC has begun weighing a fresh funding round that would value the artificial intelligence developer at more than $900 billion, according to people familiar with the matter, potentially leapfrogging its longtime rival OpenAI as the world’s most valuable AI startup.

item.title: Brazil Central Bank Trims Interest Rate to 14.5% as Economy Slows and Inflation Firms
item.description: Brazil’s central bank cut its key interest rate by a quarter point for a second straight meeting, even as inflation accelerates and signs of economic resilience emerge amid a raft of stimulus measures ahead of October’s presidential election.

item.title: Bessent Says Powell Staying On Is ‘Violation’ of All Fed Norms
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent criticized Jerome Powell for his decision to stay on the Federal Reserve Board after he steps down as the US central bank’s chair, saying it amounts to a break with Fed tradition.

item.title: US Seeks to Deploy Hypersonic Missile For the First Time Against Iran
item.description: US Central Command has asked to send the Army’s long-delayed Dark Eagle hypersonic missile to the Middle East for possible use against Iran, seeking a longer-range system to hit ballistic-missile launchers deep inside the country.

item.title: India’s Homegrown $1 Billion High-Speed Trading Unicorn Goes Global
item.description: Graviton is expanding in Singapore and London as competition and regulatory hurdles build in its home market.

item.title: US House Probes Airbnb, Anysphere’s Use of Chinese AI Models
item.description: House Republicans are investigating American technology companies’ use of Chinese artificial intelligence models, part of a broader push by Congress to limit US national security risks and to counter Beijing in the global AI race.

item.title: The Fed Officials Who Dissented at Powell's Last Meeting as Chair
item.description: Federal Reserve officials left interest rates unchanged, but revealed a growing divide over the outlook for policy.

item.title: Amazon Reports Biggest Cloud Sales Jump Since 2022 on AI Demand
item.description: Amazon.com Inc.’s cloud unit posted its fastest quarterly growth in more than three years, bolstered by new data center capacity and an increasing slice of business from Anthropic and OpenAI.

item.title: BP and Venezuela Sign Pact to Explore for Offshore Gas
item.description: BP Plc and Venezuela agreed on a deal to explore for natural gas offshore as the South American country’s energy revival gathers pace following the US capture of Nicolas Maduro.

item.title: Alphabet Sales Beat Estimates on Google Cloud, AI Customers
item.description: Alphabet Inc. reported quarterly revenue and profit that beat projections, fueled by strong growth in its cloud computing unit, signaling that the internet giant’s unprecedented investments in AI infrastructure are beginning to pay off.

item.title: Meta Shares Plunge on Escalating Concerns Over AI Spending Spree
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. shares slid after the company raised its spending outlook for the year, reigniting fears that the historic levels of investment it’s making to catch up in the artificial intelligence race won’t pay off.

item.title: Microsoft’s Cloud Growth Fails to Ease Investors’ AI Concerns
item.description: Microsoft Corp.’s cloud business reported growth that narrowly beat analysts’ estimates, disappointing investors concerned that the company isn’t fully capitalizing on demand for AI services.

item.title: Qualcomm Gives Weak Forecast After Smartphone Market Slumps
item.description: Qualcomm Inc., the largest maker of smartphone processors, gave a weak forecast for the current period, adding to evidence that an industrywide shortage of memory chips is hurting the phone business.
